web.description: 1. LoveStream: This is a live streaming platform built specifically for virtual weddings. · 2. Simply Eloped: Planning to elope, let Simply ...
web.markdown:
top of page

Search

The coronavirus pandemic made many realize that they can do so much virtually,

even at a wedding. It is not uncommon to hear about a virtual wedding event coming

up these days. Many wedding organizers have grown accustomed to this fact and

now eagerly ask their clients if they have any virtual wedding plans.

Everything about a wedding can be done online now, including the venue, vendors, officiant, fashion, and more. A good reason for this is that live streaming is a simple, very affordable way to include guests who couldn't physically attend your wedding. And the wedding

streaming app/platforms are here to make it more appropriate. It is your choice to be

as elaborate as you want or make it a simple virtual wedding. Your live streaming

platform can match whichever style you want.

Wedding streaming apps or platforms can be divided into two - full-service media

and DIY platforms, depending on the uses of the forum. This division is not absolute

as one can have the qualities of being a full-service platform yet have DIY features.

The full-service media come as mainly SaaS products, and some of them can be

pretty pricey. They come with all the bells and whistles to make your occasion a

great one. In contrast, the DIY platforms are more traditional and found in our regular

social media and communication apps. These platforms are less polished but are

almost free.

Here are the best ten wedding live streaming platforms we have found to help you

share your wedding with friends and family in different parts of the world.

1\. LoveStream:

This is a live streaming platform built specifically for virtual weddings. LoveStream has all the features you need to stream your wedding effectively. There is an easy, simple set-up, one-click streaming access so everyone anywhere in the world can join, different options for multiple camera views, guest books, and more.

2\. Simply Eloped:

Planning to elope, let Simply Eloped help you. This service prides itself in helping you have a private wedding in a great location and then stream the wedding to your family back home. You have little to no guests, and you can stay anywhere around the world. They help you choose an exotic and also with the technology for streaming the wedding. Simply Eloped can also record your small, private wedding so you can show your grandkids.

3\. Wedfuly:

This is another wedding streaming platform that collaborates with the video meeting platform Zoom. It offers two packages: a 2hours live interactive and a 1hours live viewing. Their offers also include MCs, e-invites, custom wedding websites, highlight reels, rental equipment, tech support, and much more.

4\. WedWed Mobile:

This platform can help you e-sign your marriage certificate and get it the same day. Here, the wedding is performed by ordained court wedding officiants that cover all US states worldwide. This wedding app tool not only helps you to live stream your event but helps you to video e-vite your friends and family.

5\. Married Livestream:

This professional live streaming and videography platform is designed to make your virtual wedding a success. Now you don't have to worry about your wedding being handled by a professional, as Married Livestream will make that happen. They provide professional

videography of your big day with high-quality audio and multiple camera angles.

6\. Powder &Pearls:

This is a video streaming service based in the UK, and they offer couples a professional live streaming & videography option. They are a cinematography team that creates live & traditional wedding videos for virtual couples.

7\. Lovecast –

This is a DIY live streaming platform that is simple and free! It is a wedding Livestream mobile app that uses text notification to alert guests that it's time to celebrate. There is no download required, and it offers live chat, easy one-click streaming access for unlimited guests, and 1080HD video quality.

8\. WedSites:

WedSites is another virtual wedding platform that makes it easy to link any of their virtual wedding events to your website. It could be a proposal, engagement party, bridal showers, and the wedding proper. All you need is to embed a link from them to our site.

9\. Joy:

This wedding website builder gives you easy access to live streaming through their wedding website by using any live streaming platform of your choice. You can use Zoom, Google Hangouts, Facebook Live, etc.

10\. Zoom:

You are probably already familiar with using Zoom for meetings and conferences. Do you know that you can use it for your virtual wedding ceremony? Yes, create & schedule an appointment, share the link and password credentials with guests, and your wedding live streaming platform is ready. You can use any webcam device to stream your wedding live. Bear in mind that the platform allows 100 participants and 40 mins for free. You can upgrade for more guests and longer calls.

Apart from the platforms mentioned above, you can use Google Hangouts, Facebook live, youtube live, and Instagram Live to stream your wedding. There you have it. Ten of the best wedding streaming platforms you can use to make your

special day a very memorable one.

## Wedding Live Streaming Company Orlando FL

# Live Stream Your Wedding

_A simple way to live stream your wedding and keep your_

_family members & loved ones connected_

* * *

##### Professional Video Gear

We have a variety of professional live streaming and video gear to stream your wedding live.

##### Custom Designed Wedding Web page

We will create a custom wedding web page for your guests to view the stream.

##### Professional Staff

Our team of professionals is here to make sure your wedding live stream goes smoothly.

[Check Availability](https://livehubevents.com/contact-us)

[Pricing](https://livehubevents.com/wedding-live-streaming-service#pricing)

## Wedding Live Streaming Company

### How Does Live Streaming a Wedding Work?

#### Request A Consultation

There are a lot of factors that can go into live streaming your wedding live. The easiest thing to do is request a free consultation so we can talk about your needs.

#### Pre-Wedding Coordination

We partner with you or your wedding planner to make sure everyone involved can easily access your wedding live stream.

#### Live Stream

#### Your Wedding

Get your wedding online quickly and easily with our wedding live streaming packages! No Stress and nothing else to think about on your wedding day.

We use advanced video and live streaming gear to broadcast your wedding! If your looking for a live stream wedding videographer to help you stream your wedding then give us a call! We include everything needed to share your wedding day, including a videographer, and professional video and audio equipment. Don't add one more thing to your wedding day to-do list, let us take care of your virtual guests.

We can live stream to your own personalized wedding website or directly to your facebook or youtube page with multi-channel streaming. We have single camera packages perfect for a remote ceremony, or a multi-camera shoot so you don't miss a thing. Most couples opt to use our own live streaming platform and stream to a custom viewing page we create for them.

Check To See If Your Wedding Date Is Available Today!

[Contact Us](https://livehubevents.com/wedding-live-streaming-service)

## Benefits Of Live Streaming Your Wedding

_There are tons of reasons to stream your wedding. From connecting to your loved ones to keeping your cost down, live streaming is a great way to share your wedding with friends and family._

Save Money

Sometimes the sheer size of the guest list can be positively daunting. You don't want to offend anyone by not inviting them. Here you've found the perfect compromise: live streaming lets them all witness your union, all while saving 100% on their travel expenses. **Fewer expenditures could mean better quality gifts!**

Stay Connected With Love Ones

By live streaming your wedding you're letting them **stay connected in real time - and safe - from afar**.

Use The Venue You Want To

Save on the reception catering expense by **downsizing your list's in-person attendees**, and live stream it instead!

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

## What our wedding live stream setup looks like

![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Do the cameras from the live stream get in the way?

No, we use a variety of camera setups depending on the venue and your needs. Our cameras have zoom lenses that allow for positioning in the back and sides of the ceremony room.

Is there a person at each camera?

No, we use tripods and multiple camera angles to make sure we can capture every moment. We may tuck cameras in a row or in the back of the room depending on how many people and the shape of the room. The setup is battery powered and easy to move. We will work with your photographer and videographer to ensure we are not interfearing with their setup.

- ![Jennifer & Septime Sep. 2020 Ceremony SHORT](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/985294344-fef9ab796e9552e50406c2cbd69dc54077f6c135dff158e25796737afd13592b-d_1280x720?&r=pad&region=us)
- ![Live Hub Events | Event Production & Live Streaming](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/1035739226-f4554806fd02b912c42b9821ecce03688b4f47c856a8f21d45e8002cb32ad713-d_1280x720?&r=pad&region=us)
- ![Stacy & Richard Highlight 10-4-2020](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/972386914-ebc1a2574c885e88eb59ac249b5dfb85d591349e82730832449523d4eb29fe69-d_1280x720?&r=pad&region=us)
- ![Zach & Elise Highlight](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/954030869-0e429604fa567d497b74f8fc793fc697737fd8c131db803da54d9765fbe76c3d-d_1280x720?&r=pad&region=us)

* * *

Virtual Wedding Toast

**Live streaming your wedding** is great but sometimes we have loved ones that we really want to see or interact with. Having **virtual guests** is a trend that many are experiencing. Maybe a best friend that can't be there, an aunt or uncle that couldn't travel, there is something about seeing and interacting with them in-person that makes everything that much more special. That is why we created an option for a **"virtual wedding toast"**. This allows the wedding couple to interact with virtual guests on a video call while allowing everyone attending in person and watching through live stream to see the interaction. Check out this video to see what our "virtual toast" add on could look like at your wedding.

Read The FAQ's

What type of cameras do you use for broadcasting?

* * *

We use a variety of different cameras depending on our client's request and budget. We have everything from GoPros to High End Video Cameras Like Red or Black Magi. However, all of our cameras will produce a good video quality for your wedding stream.

Our ceremony is not amplified. Will our guests be able to hear us?

* * *

Absolutely. First & foremost we are a professional Wedding videography company. We utilize multiple wireless microphones so everything will look right & sound right. Even if your ceremony is not amplified, your guest watching your live stream at home will be able to hear every word. We also monitor the audio quality during the video stream. Need a basic sound system setup? We can cover that too! Ask us about our [sound system rentals](https://livehubevents.com/audio-visual-production).

Does your packages cover only the ceremony? Can we film/stream additional "main events"?

* * *

We can cover any type of event you would like. Our base live streaming and wedding video packages mentioned above cover only the ceremony which is usually around 40 minutes. We do offer additional streaming and filming options like a First Dance, and/or toasts, dances with parents, and/or cake cutting. Those additional formalities will be included in the highlight video. If your interested in pricing then please contact us for a custom quote.

Can I just rent your equipment and stream it myself?

* * *

The short answer is no. We offer a turnkey streaming services so our clients don't have to worry about designating an operator, learning the platform, or worrying about if the camera battery is going to die during the wedding ceremony. We leave them to focus on what they need to do and let us worry about all the technical stuff. Let us live stream your wedding ceremony and/or reception on your wedding day.

Do I need internet at my location to use your services?

* * *

While this does help us keep your cost down you are NOT required to have an internet connection at your location. We can utilize different technologies to get stable internet to your location suitable for broadcasting. The speeds that are required would be dependent upon what quality wedding stream you need, which of course effects the price of your package.

What Browsers /devices are supported?

* * *

Our streaming platform is supported on all major desktop/laptop browsers including Chrome, Safari, Internet Explorer and Firefox. Your friends and family can watch on whatever device they want provided they have a good internet connection.

How many viewers can simultaneously watch my wedding?

* * *

We utilize a content delivery network that ensures all viewers can view your live stream with minimal delay and the highest possible quality. The possibilities are endless if we utilize facebook live or youtube live as well. You can share your wedding to as many people as you want. Included in all of our packages is support up to 200 people.

What type of speed will people need to view the stream?

* * *

For most viewers a download speed of 5 Mbps should be sufficient enough for an HD stream. If their internet connectivity is slower than that then they will need to choose a lower quality stream to ensure a proper viewing experience.

Can I Re-Watch the broadcast? Is it possible to download the video footage?

* * *

Yes, after your wedding is complete you will receive a highlight video of your ceremony as well as a complete video upload of your wedding.

I already have a wedding videographer. Can you work with them?

* * *

Yes! Our platform allows us to use a variety of different cameras to broadcast. As long as your videographer has any of our compatible cameras then we can work with them to broadcast your ceremony and or wedding.

What if my DJ is already providing microphones?

* * *

We can work with your wedding planner and other vendors during the planning process to see what is needed to make sure your wedding is completely covered. We use a combination of external microphones, audio sources, and wireless microphones to make sure we capture all the audio we need. However, because every wedding is different we already include in the price for us to bring this gear because not all vendors may be up to our standards.

What are the terms of your contract?

* * *

We require a signed contract and a non-refundable retainer of $75 to book your wedding date. The remainder of the balance is due 5 days prior to your wedding day to make sure you don't have to stress about payment.

We use cookies to ensure that we give you the best experience on our website. If you continue to use this site we will assume that you are happy with it. [Ok](https://livehubevents.com/wedding-live-streaming-service#)

web.description: LoveStream is the easiest way to live stream your wedding. With personalized onboarding and full support throughout planning and on wedding day.
web.markdown:
![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/057186d4-0e07-4261-908c-cedf2f0e5ea4/New+homepage+mockups+%281600+x+1080+px%29+%286%29.png)

# Live stream your wedding. With experienced pros.

You don’t want to botch your wedding live stream with a DIY option. Trust our team of experts to do it for you.

![live stream wedding depiction](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/2a9317c1-3d87-48bc-87af-f23ae1375dab/wedding-live-stream.png)

[Book a Free Consultation](https://calendly.com/d/hj4-st2-xfc)

## Built for weddings. Not for work calls.

### LoveStream is the _only_ full-service wedding live stream company actually built for weddings.

[How It Works](https://www.lovestream.io/how-it-works)

Unable to load video. Try again later.

### **Sure, we live stream weddings. But we also offer** **real human support** **, build you a** **custom website** **, meet with you virtually to** **plan details** **, test equipment during a** **tech rehearsal** **, and** **produce your wedding remotely** **on wedding day.**

### **We do it all. So you don’t have to.**

Hundreds of happy couples.

Item 1 of 13

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/a33d97c0-06a7-49a3-ae12-e5558a182ed0/3.png)

Highlight - Dylan and Whitney

### This Broadcast is Unavailable

## A team of live stream experts producing every wedding.

With a team of wedding planners and live stream experts, we **combine the best software with the human element** to produce reliable, seamless wedding live streams.

Prior to the wedding, our team helps to troubleshoot internet issues, create a streaming timeline and provide all the best practices you need for a successful stream.

On wedding day, **think of your producer as a TV director**. They will switch between camera one, two and three to choose the best angle at any moment for your virtual guests.

They also handle anything that could go wrong - from alerting guests you are running behind to extending your stream to make sure no one misses your first kiss.

We handle everything. So you don’t have to.

[View Packages](https://www.lovestream.io/package-pricing)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/82555067-fd57-428d-b47e-bd1af4201e37/4.png)

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/01e8d613-d280-4dfb-a68a-00d5ff26b221/LoveStream+-+phone+positions+device+setup.png)

## **Multiple camera angles ensure every special moment is captured.**

Our multi-camera wedding live stream packages include a remote producer who **mixes and matches the best camera angle and best audio** so virtual guests always see and hear the best parts (check out our audio versus phone audio [here](https://www.lovestream.io/package-pricing)).

Stream from **devices you already own** (like iPhones and Androids) or rent ours. For a third of the price of a videographer.

_Example of where LoveStream devices can be placed._

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/82555067-fd57-428d-b47e-bd1af4201e37/4.png)

![wedding live stream website](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/3cba4cfc-03cb-47da-abf4-9a9f37be8d58/wedding-livestream-website.png)

## Guests can watch your wedding in real-time. With a view better than the front row.

Guests simply **click the link** to your wedding website, **press play**, and they are able to help you **celebrate your wedding live**, as if they were there.

No downloading an app, logging in or waiting for an admin to admit them.

As a **one-way wedding live stream**, guests don’t have to worry about muting themselves or granting camera access. And if they have questions? Our team is there to support them.

[Book consultation](https://calendly.com/d/hj4-st2-xfc)

We put in the work. So you can enjoy your wedding.

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638995646320-GC8GQOHJWQALZJRVIOHE/4.png)







## Custom website.





Our team builds a custom wedding website to serve as your virtual venue. Guests can sign your virtual guest book, drop well-wishes in the live chat, buy goodies from your registry and RSVP.

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638995765881-GX2A0CG6XLLYYZBL22JJ/3.png)







## Onboarding support.





Our team of wedding planning experts will be in touch within one business day to go over all the details of your wedding live stream, set up your planning call and assist with any questions.

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1646690053422-YQ3DBLZJE08EHZ3JF39C/Icons%2C+Images%2C+Mockups+LoveStream+and+Bustld+%2816%29.png)







## Production Team





A lot can happen during a wedding live stream. Your production team will help troubleshoot issues so virtual guests don’t miss an important moment of your wedding - like your first kiss!

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638995716810-7EFFJXYEN34D4STPFBHD/5.png)







## Easy set up.





Set up in less than 10 minutes with the help of your remote producer. Choose either a trusted friend or hire our team to provide a person on-site (if desired) in 325+ cities worldwide.

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638996107735-BYSMWNIYJ6ZWE2Z2BXMQ/Untitled+%28750+x+750+px%29+%281%29.png)







## One-click guest access.





With no guest limit, loved ones go to your wedding live stream website, press play and they’re watching your wedding in real-time. It’s so easy, grandma can do it (and just in case she can’t… we are there to help!).

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1639165405286-DMAM7G0XKQE1PLNH7K28/New+design+-+icons.png)







## Rich audio for at-home guests.





With our rentable broadcast-quality microphones, your at-home guests can hear every important moment. No conference call-style microphones or questionable phone audio here. Want to hear the difference our mics make? [Click here](https://www.lovestream.io/using-mics-samples).

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638996131885-KLAGF8ZSSF5JOKB00BEW/8.png)







## Watch live or later.





Your wedding live stream works like on-demand. Guests can watch live or watch on their own time after the wedding airs.

- ![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/1638996177099-XZNFVTUTF6285J5I8QEW/9.png)







## Keep your video.





Within three business days, our team will deliver a folder of all your keepsakes including a downloadable copy of your LoveStream, live chat and virtual guest book. No waiting over a month for a videographer.


![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5eebbbd8ea63111fa1b972f7/dd4d25ea-12ab-4883-b928-470610c5a799/New+homepage+mockups+%281600+x+1080+px%29+%2812%29.png)

### **Missed kisses, internet failures, dead air and guest login issues are just a few things the LoveStream team makes sure doesn’t happen. Live stream your wedding without the stress.**

[Chat With Our Team](https://calendly.com/d/hj4-st2-xfc)

## Questions + Answers.

Have questions? We have answers.

LoveStream is _the_ easiest way to live stream your wedding. With personalized onboarding and full support throughout planning and on wedding day, our production team makes sure you are never left in the dark.

[More Qs? Book a Consultation](https://calendly.com/d/hj4-st2-xfc)

- #### What does a LoveStream producer do?






LoveStream producers tell the story of your wedding day through live camera switching, adding banners and music to your stream, and running your stream as orchestrated by you and our planning team in advance. Think of them like a TV producer - choosing camera one or two, rolling video and adding announcements to your broadcast. Our producers are skilled at creating an experience for your online guests.

- #### How is LoveStream production different than a DIY app?






Our production packages combine the best software with the human element to create a reliable live stream for your once-in-a-lifetime event.



Our team of wedding planning pros have 20+ years experience in the industry. They will help guide you through how to prepare for your live stream and plan your stream for the best guest experience.



Our live stream tech experts will also provide assistance – helping you to test internet connectivity, find the best camera angles and ensuring you have everything you need to successfully live stream your wedding.



On wedding day, you and your guests are never alone. Your setup person will be supported by our remote producers (or we can handle setup for you!) to troubleshoot any day-of issues to ensure the best stream possible.



Don’t leave your wedding live stream to chance. Let our pros guide you on capturing this moment for every guest, anywhere, to see.



Still think DIY is the right fit for you? Our DIY “Stream in a Box” package includes all the equipment you will need to capture your event, as well as a “How To” guide with all our helpful tips and tricks to ensure your DIY stream goes well.

- #### How is LoveStream different than Zoom?






LoveStream is a one-way, HD live stream service built for weddings with no guest limit.



One-way streaming allows guests to watch the ceremony as if they were there and prevents guests from interrupting your wedding live stream by unmuting themselves and taking over the screen.



There is also no complicated login or download process. If guests can click a link, they can watch your live stream.



We utilize 1080p broadcast quality technology to deliver a clearer video product for your guests ( [see the comparison here](https://www.lovestream.io/lovestream-vs-zoom-examples)). The video player also works like on-demand so guests can watch it later if they miss the original broadcast time.

- #### Can I test the live stream before my event?






Of course! As wedding professionals, we know how important it is to be prepared (and have a plan B in place - that’s why our producers are here!). Each couple will schedule a time to review best practices, review camera angles and test audio with our pros prior to wedding day.



You’ll never be alone trying to figure out how to fit technology seamlessly into your in-person celebration.

- #### Do I get a copy of my wedding video?






Of course! Immediately after your wedding live stream ends, your video will be viewable on your wedding website.



Within three business days, you’ll be emailed a downloadable single-file copy of your stream as it aired along with your virtual guest book and live chat transcript.

- #### What equipment do we need?






The short answer: Streaming device(s) and a tripod.



Streaming devices can be a smartphone, tablet or laptop that you own or you can rent ours. We also rent heavy-duty tripods that are great for both indoor and outdoor weddings.



For the best audio, we recommend renting our broadcast-quality, wireless clip-on microphone kit ( [hear the difference it makes here](https://www.lovestream.io/using-mics-samples)).



You can view all our add-ons [here](https://shop.lovestream.io/collections/add-ons). Equipment rentals are included in our Elite and White Glove packages.

- #### Do you recommend a mic for the live stream?






While it’s not required, we highly recommend renting our wireless, broadcast-quality microphone (included in our Elite and White Glove packages). Audio is mission critical to a successful wedding live stream - after all your guests want to hear you, not just see you. Phone quality microphones are simply not as good. You can compare the audio [here](https://www.lovestream.io/using-mics-samples).

- #### Who sets up on wedding day?






You will choose a trusted person on-site to help set up on wedding day. For this, you have two options:



**1) Hire an on-site assistant.**



LoveStream can provide an on-site assistant starting at $350. **Most** couples pay $350 for this service, but based on location and individual needs, this price may increase.



_Included in our White Glove package._



**2) Choose your own day-of contact.**



If choosing your own day-of contact (what 95% of our couples opt for), we typically recommend a plus-one of someone in the wedding party because they will be on-site all day, but won’t be busy taking pictures. You can also choose a trusted friend or family member to set up.



Set up is easy and **takes less than 10 minutes**. Typical set up involves clicking a link and placing the streaming device(s) on tripods. LoveStream rents tripods and microphones that can help increase the quality of your stream ( [listen here](https://www.lovestream.io/using-mics-samples)).

- #### How many guests can I have? Can they watch from anywhere?






You can have an unlimited number of guests watch your live stream wedding - with no extra fees for more guests.



Your wedding live stream works like on-demand. If guests are in different time zones, they can come to your website and watch at a time that is convenient for them.



Plus, we’ve streamed all around the world (168 countries and counting!). Distance is no issue for LoveStream.

- #### Is my wedding website private?






Yes! We hide your link from search engines so only guests who have been given the link can access your wedding website to watch the stream. You can also add a password to your page for an additional level of security.

- #### How do I purchase?






If you have more questions or want to see more examples, book a free consultation with our co-founder [**_here_**](https://www.lovestream.io/book-a-meeting) **_._**


Unable to load video. Try again later.

web.title: Full-Service Wedding Livestream Platforms Your Venue Should Know
web.description: LoveStream, built by the wedding pros at Bustld, this easy-to-use streaming service is like the Rolls Royce of live-streaming platforms.
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://hawthorncreative.com/blog/full-service-wedding-livestream-platforms-with-which-your-venue-should-be-familiar/#content)

![Full-Service Wedding Livestream Platforms Your Venue Should Be Familiar With](https://hawthorncreative.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Full-Service-Wedding-Livestream-Platforms-Your-Venue-Should-Be-Familiar-With.jpg)

Livestreaming video has absolutely flourished during the coronavirus pandemic – and for good reason. Whether you are the one creating it or consuming it, streaming content has been a prime source of escapism. But if you think the livestream boom will quickly be abandoned once larger in-person gatherings – such as weddings – are once again safe and acceptable, you probably have a different reality coming.

According to [an Insider September article](https://www.insider.com/virtual-wedding-planning-coronavirus-pandemic-2020-8) covering the recent rise in livestreaming weddings, planners are predicting that livestreaming is here to stay long past this pandemic. Why? Well, why not? It provides another added benefit – a safeguard even – for couples to ensure that their loved ones will be able to experience their special day. And as the pandemic has shown us, we’re often living in an uncertain world, leading to reasons someone (or multiple someones) can’t make the wedding, whether that’s an elderly grandparent, a pregnant friend, relatives who live too far away, or even natural disasters stepping in to spoil things (in fact, 2011’s Hurricane Irene is what led to the creation of popular streaming service, LoveStream, after one-third of the guests couldn’t make the app founder’s wedding as a result of the storm).

**As for what this means for you, the wedding venue, it can’t hurt to make streaming options a part of your recommendations to couples now (as many wedding planners already are).** Especially, as you’ll undoubtedly be seeing future couples coming to you with the question of “how to stream a wedding?” or “what’s the best way to livestream a wedding?” While the most ideal option would be to build your own venue-branded full-service streaming service right into your wedding package, just simply knowing what’s out there in terms of platforms and technologies, the different features, and how they could benefit certain prospective couples can go a long way in positioning you as the venue they need.

## For Your Couples Who Want All the (Wedding) Bells & Whistles

### [LoveStream](https://www.lovestream.io/)

Built by the wedding pros at [Bustld](https://bustld.com/), this easy-to-use streaming service is like the Rolls Royce of live-streaming platforms. That’s because it doesn’t just provide user-friendly, one-click HD streaming access for your client’s unlimited guests (no downloading necessary), but it offers a customized website that can include a live chat, virtual guestbook, Spotify playlist, and specialty cocktail recipe. There’s also a built-in production team to control video feeds at important moments during your client’s day, like switching between camera angles or featuring a remote guest’s reading for a ceremony. And proof that this particular service is the crème de la crème (and that this trend isn’t going away): They have had wedding inquiries for dates as far out as October 2021.

Intrigued? Lovestream also offers a [venue and vendor affiliate program](https://www.lovestream.io/virtual-wedding-vendor-affiliate), which provides you with a 10% discount code for your clients, a custom link with 120-day cookie tracking, promotional marketing materials, and even a 10% commission paid monthly for each LoveStream purchase completed using your link.

### SOLUTIONS

## Eye-Catching Video is a Marketing Must

Turn browsing into booking with videography that shows off your unique venue experience

[This Way to Better Video](https://hawthorncreative.com/solutions/photography-videography/)

## For Your Couples Who Want More Interactive Elements

### [Wedfuly](https://wedfuly.com/)

Collaborating with video meeting platform [Zoom](https://zoom.us/), Wedfuly – previously an online wedding planning service that has shifted its focus almost entirely to virtual wedding livestreams – pulled off its first virtual wedding in March of 2020. What’s particularly unique about this platform is the way it leverages different features of Zoom to makes events interactive. For example, coordinators use screen sharing throughout the entire event for visuals including videos and slideshows, the “raise hand” feature is great for open-floor toasts, and it even places guests into Zoom breakout rooms that function as reception tables, so the bride and groom can then pop into these rooms and speak with smaller groups individually, just like they would at a regular wedding.

## For Your Couples Who Want Their Livestream to Look Ultra-Professional

### [Married LiveStream](https://www.marriedlivestream.com/)

This Pacific Northwest-based videography company makes livestreaming look, well, like a professionally constructed video shoot – just all managed to be done live and in-person. Multiple cameras and high-quality audio help tell the full story of the couples’ wedding day from afar, from the start of the ceremony to the last lingerers on the dancefloor at the end of the reception. Not to mention, the full recording is also available to clients quick – within 24 hours. See an example of a [previously recorded livestream here](https://www.marriedlivestream.com/live-streams).

## For Your Eloping Couples Who Still Want a Few Virtual Witnesses

### [Simply Eloped](https://simplyeloped.com/virtual-weddings/)

Simply Eloped is a wedding planning service dedicated to making eloping easy for couples. So, as other livestreaming providers came into the picture as a result of the pandemic, they, too, began offering virtual ceremonies performed through Zoom, Skype, FaceTime, or the couple’s other preferred video conferencing tools. Multi-tiered packages include virtual tech-support, officiation services, even a one-hour photography portrait session after the ceremony.

### CASE STUDY

## Red Ridge Receptions

A boutique website and brochure for a breathtaking venue.

[See the Journey](https://hawthorncreative.com/work/casestudies/red-ridge-receptions/)

## Other DIY Wedding Livestream Platforms Worthy to Note for Them

They may not be full-service, but they still are getting plenty of use by couples nationwide.

- [Wedsites](https://wedsites.com/virtual-weddings) – A wedding website builder that allows couples to embed links to their virtual wedding right on their wedding website.
- [Lovecast](https://www.lovecastapp.com/) – A mobile app that features one-click streaming access for guests in HD video, plus a live chat feature.
- [Joy](https://help.withjoy.com/knowledge-base/how-to-host-your-virtual-wedding-with-joy) – A wedding website builder that supports live streaming through pretty much any platform out there: Zoom, YouTube, Google Meet, Facebook Live, and more.

### RELATED READING

_Our team of designers, writers, and strategists are at your digital disposal._

- [10 Steps to Nailing an Original Wedding Venue Photo Shoot](https://hawthorncreative.com/blog/10-steps-to-nailing-an-original-venue-photo-shoot-for-wedding-venue-marketers/)

This free e-book will help supercharge your social media ROI

web.description: Live Stream Your Wedding! We use the best in streaming technology to create a quality broadcast that will impress your highly critical family member.
web.markdown:
_Press enter to begin your search_

![](https://karmaeventproductions.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/slider-piece.png)

![](https://karmaeventproductions.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/filigree-sides-left-purple.jpg)

## Live Stream Your Wedding!

We use the best in streaming technology to create a quality broadcast that will impress your highly critical family member. Your family, grandparents, and friends will love sitting in the virtual front row of your wedding ceremony. They will be able to watch live as you share your vows, your first dance, and every other important moment.

- Professional audio and video streaming
- Multi- camera angles
- Live stream to Facebook, YouTube, Zoom, & more
- Or streamed to private website
- Digital download recording for you to keep

![](https://karmaeventproductions.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/filigree-sides-right-purple.jpg)

Wedding Live-Stream Demo from The Clayton House Scottsdale - YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Wedding Live-Stream Demo from The Clayton House Scottsdale](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kl6IzdwBrxo) [Latest Craze Productions](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCKkkE3VN_FySSxRP5-fa0gQ)

Latest Craze Productions1.1K subscribers

[Watch on](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kl6IzdwBrxo)

Example of live stream wedding setup at [The Clayton House](https://www.theclaytonvenues.com/the-clayton-house/)

## Professional Production

- **_We Come To You_** – Our portable setup allows us to stream anywhere at any time. We also have portable power and internet as backups to ensure a perfect live stream at any location.
- **_High Quality Audio_** – Our wireless microphones capture crystal clear audio which dramatically improves the stream quality. We can also pull audio from your DJ or sound technician.
- **_Quick & Easy Setup_** – We can have your live stream up and going within 20 minutes of arriving. Our setup is also compact which means your guests will hardly notice that we are even there.
- **_High Definition Video_** – We can stream up to 1080p (HD) with our cameras. The live view will switch between our different cameras to provide a front row movie-like experience.

# The Creative Toolkit for Marketers

## [Top 5 Virtual Event and Webinar Streaming Services](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/top-virtual-event-webinar-live-streaming-services)

Chelsea Sassara

- Post Button


![Woman sitting at desk attending a live virtual webinar on her computer](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/hs-fs/hubfs/linkedin-virtual-event-streaming-services-vmg-studios.jpg?width=1136&name=linkedin-virtual-event-streaming-services-vmg-studios.jpg)

Virtual events, live-streaming, and webinars are on the rise thanks to impressive technology and online, global access. This type of deliverable content is also seeing a spike in popularity due to a shifting work-from-home culture amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

There are many benefits to hosting virtual events, including the ability for people around the world to attend and the interactive features that allow attendees to participate and collaborate with one another and with the presented content.

Deciding to host a virtual event – whether it’s a conference, product launch, training course, or TED Talk-type lecture – may be an easy one but figuring out _how_ to do so probably isn’t as simple.

A big part of the success of a virtual event is where it’s hosted. The streaming service or platform. And there are _dozens_ to choose from.

As a creative marketing agency that specializes in video, animation, and dynamic presentations, we are learning the ins and outs of virtual event production alongside many of you and have conducted research to better understand this event strategy.

This article will lay out the top 5 virtual event streaming services, in no particular order, based on customer reviews, services offered, features, and pricing.

## **1\. BigMarker**

[BigMarker](https://www.bigmarker.com/) is a video platform for webinars, summits, and virtual conferences. On its website, BigMarker boasts its technology which “helps people turn dull monologues into engaging conversations that activate audiences and build stronger relationships.”

- _Services_
  - Live webinars
  - Web summits and series
  - Live streams and webcasts
  - Automated webinars
  - On-demand video
  - Webinar marketing

One of the main selling points for BigMarker is its webinars run in the cloud through a web browser meaning no download is necessary – something your attendees will likely appreciate.

- _Features_
  - Embeddable video player which is ideal for [pre-recorded content.](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/pre-recorded-content-versus-live-streaming-virtual-events)
  - Lead forms
  - Polls
  - Q&A
  - Stream to Facebook and YouTube

Some of BigMarker’s clients, according to their website, include Google, Samsung, McDonald’s, Panasonic, Wells Fargo, and Waze.

- _Pricing_
  - Starter
    - $79/mo
    - Up to 100 attendees
  - Elite
    - $159/mo
    - Up to 500 attendees
  - Summit
    - $299/mo
    - Up to 1,000 attendees
  - White label
    - Needs to contact BigMarker for pricing options
    - Up to 10,000 attendees

BigMarker is transparent about their [pricing options and the features that come with each tier on their website.](https://www.bigmarker.com/pricing) They also offer a free 7-day trial.

## **2\. vFairs**

[vFairs](https://www.vfairs.com/) highlights its ability to give you “all the ingredients to host a successful virtual event.” vFairs supports virtual events with audiences of scale and reporting capabilities. Its services are broken down into 3 main categories: organizations, corporations, and universities.

- _Services_
  - Virtual conferences
  - Job and career fairs
  - Trade shows
  - Onboarding fairs
  - Alumni networking events

vFairs offers 24/7 global customer support, multi-lingual support, and its products are mobile compliant and accessible.

- _Features_
  - Custom registration forms
  - Virtual lobby for attendees
  - Information desk
  - Interactivity tools
  - Q&A
  - Polls

Clients include, but aren’t limited to, Nestle, American Airlines, Six Flags, T-Mobile, and Ford.

- _Pricing_

vFairs does not explicitly list pricing options on its website, but according to one of its educational guides, [the cost of a virtual event starts around $8,000](https://www.vfairs.com/the-ultimate-guide-to-virtual-career-fairs/).

vFairs has an extensive [learning hub](https://www.vfairs.com/resources/) of educational content on their website to learn more about virtual events.

## **3\. Adobe Connect**

[Adobe Connect](https://www.adobe.com/products/adobeconnect.html) supports businesses including enterprise-level corporations, government organizations, and schools with web conferencing capabilities. Its website highlights its [multilayered security approach](https://www.adobe.com/products/adobeconnect/security.html) – a big selling point for some of their clients including the U.S. Department of Defense.

- _Services_
  - Webinars
  - Meeting rooms
  - Live virtual classrooms

Adobe Connect offers [webinars, daily demos, and on-demand recordings](https://events-ar.adobeconnect.com/content/connect/c1/655548740/en/events/catalog.html?folder-id=789961994&from-origin=onlineevents.adobeconnect.com&OWASP_CSRFTOKEN=b6606240bfb5e5b161cb79e7cf49f3b4e53cedee6ebc3cde79fe0e9fe7605a82) on how to use their services.

- _Features_
  - Screen/document sharing
  - Polls
  - Q&A
  - Chat
  - Rich media sharing
  - Customizable layouts

Other clients of Adobe Connect, besides the U.S. Department of Defense, include SAP, Toshiba, and Johns Hopkins University.

- _Pricing_
  - Webinars
    - $130/mo for up to 100 participants
    - $470/mo for up to 500 participants
    - $580/mo for up to 1,000 participants
    - Must contact the sales department for requests of more than 1,000 participants
  - Meetings
    - $50/mo
    - Meeting with up to 25 participants
  - Learning
    - $370/mo
    - Virtual classroom with up to 200 participants

If you’d like to get a sneak peek at Adobe Connect, it does offer a [free 90-day trial period](https://www.adobe.com/products/adobeconnect/trial.html)for meetings which can host up for 25 participants.

## **4\. Livestream (from Vimeo)**

Vimeo is a popular video platform so it’s no surprise that it offers end-to-end streaming solutions. [Livestream](https://livestream.com/) boasts unlimited viewership for most users, as well as options for brand control including adding your logo or sponsors to your stream.

- _Services_
  - Virtual events
  - Conferences
  - Meetings
  - Trainings

Livestream utilizes Vimeo’s internal video gallery which is a centralized portal where you can publish, manage, control access, and archive all your corporate videos in one place.

- _Features_
  - Live polls
  - Q&A
  - Audience chat
  - Analytics
  - Video management
  - Live production

Livestream has a long list of clients including LinkedIn, lululemon, Toyota, Sony, and Spotify.

- _Pricing_
  - Premium
    - $75/mo
    - Ideal for small organizations
  - Enterprise
    - Must contact sales for pricing options
    - Ideal for larger organizations

Users can also stream their content to Facebook Live, YouTube, Periscope, and Twitch with Livestream’s Simulcast dashboard.

## **5\. WebinarJam**

[WebinarJam](https://home.webinarjam.com/index) sells itself as the online live virtual events streaming platform for anyone: anyone who is looking to connect with an audience, sell a product, coach a program, etc. It offers hybrid webinars, meaning you could [play pre-recorded content during a live webinar.](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/pre-recorded-content-versus-live-streaming-virtual-events)

- _Services_
  - Live webinars
  - Pre-recorded webinars
  - Recurring webinar series
  - Event page builders

WebinarJam uses cloud-based broadcasting technology and allows up to 5,000 participants in a single presentation.

- _Features_
  - Live chat
  - Polls and surveys
  - Video injections
  - Automated recordings
  - Mobile friendly

WebinarJam anticipates the fear of failing technology with a “panic button” feature. If you’re detecting technical issues, you can click the panic button and the system will immediately boot up a new live room while automatically transferring all the presenters and attendees into it.

- _Pricing_
  - Basic
    - $499/year
    - Unlimited webinars
    - Up to 500 attendees
    - 2 hours max duration
  - Professional
    - $699/year
    - Unlimited webinars
    - Up to 2,000 attendees
    - 3 hours max duration
    - Panic button
  - Enterprise
    - $999/year
    - Unlimited webinars
    - Up to 5,000 attendees
    - 4 hours max duration
    - Panic button

WebinarJam also hosts a library of [on-demand webinars](https://ondemand.webinarjam.com/#_ga=2.154059640.1782392035.1586989965-690255628.1586989965) from more than 3,000 creators spanning an array of topics such as fitness, finance, parenting, cooking, gaming, beauty, home and gardening, and much more.

## **Finding the Right Virtual Event Streaming Platform**

If you’re new to holding virtual events and live webinars, finding the right streaming platform can be overwhelming due in part to the long list of options. However, finding the right fit doesn’t necessarily need to be the first decision you make when planning a virtual event. In fact, it probably _shouldn’t be_ the first decision.

Start organizing your event by identifying your budget, the number of attendees you expect, whether you want to sprinkle in pre-recorded content, and how you want to interact with the audience, and you will be able to whittle down the list of options.

If you need help outlining the elements of your event, download our free virtual event creative brief to start planning today.

[VIRTUAL EVENT CREATIVE BRIEF](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/cs/c/?cta_guid=c0df2064-41d6-4767-94ca-6a489e8c3140&signature=AKRbUmHbp6eHTGsLCj3PvnEqh9Fxqk7Bug&portal_id=6120577&pageId=28833869402&placement_guid=c730c274-eabd-46e3-9b87-e235fb78c63e&click=440f263d-e884-4d10-9bf3-04bcca372a6f&redirect_url=AMCV9XnIvq69faQsr8el9ONda7kFJMiwSfRCpTb_NaWvDAhbFdmOcRvqrsdj2bOORyfZP6UU0WxeTPPLLg1PRZmPltr20O5bEckFXCPbRDRxebr1qj6iwBzKUbdbUWvnnYQP2FqWVZAcpGiJ4q0MeHFKepaNiTBp9gOr5Bz2R5Fn66TJMMQ2yOI&hsutk=&canon=https%3A%2F%2Fblog.vmgstudios.com%2Ftop-virtual-event-webinar-live-streaming-services&ts=1776303043938 "VIRTUAL EVENT CREATIVE BRIEF")

Now let's start by talking about what equipment you're gonna need for your wedding ceremony live stream. For the sake of this example, we're gonna be fancy, and I'm gonna be using three cameras for this live stream to really show you some fun things that you can do. You're also going to need at least one wireless microphone system that I recommend putting on the efficient for the ceremony. Now in the past to run a ceremony live stream, you would need a switcher box that would handle all of the connections from these cameras and switch between them. The switcher would then run into a laptop, they would either be connected to WiFi at the wedding venue, or I've also run ethernet cable several hundred feet at weddings before to guarantee a better connection while also connected to WiFi for redundancy. As you can see, running a wedding-filled live stream has been a lot of work in the past. You need cameras, a switcher, laptop, internet, but what if you could simplify all that? What if you could make it dramatically easier by removing multiple required components and replacing them with just one device? That's where YoloBox comes in. Specifically, this is the YoloBox Extreme, but they sell multiple different devices depending on the size of the live stream that you want to do and how many cameras you want to use, et cetera. I will link to my YoloBox recommendations for different wedding sizes down in the video description. The main thing that you need to know is that this YoloBox is capable of handling the job of the switcher, the laptop, and the internet, and this makes live streaming a wedding dramatically simpler. Now that we've covered the equipment that you're gonna need, let's talk about setting it all up for a wedding. First off, you're gonna want to set up a home base of sorts where you can run the YoloBox. So instead of a table, I recommend mounting it to a tripod or even a light stand, which will be unobtrusive and enable you to have the screen right at eye level while you are using it. Now, if you're wanting to run a live stream with just one camera, you could even clamp the YoloBox to one of the legs of the tripod that your camera is on. Then to connect your camera to the YoloBox, all you will need is an HDMI cable that will run from the camera to the YoloBox. One thing that's great about this setup being so small and portable is that the closer the YoloBox is to your camera, the shorter of an HDMI cable that you will need to connect to your camera. Of course, though, if you want to use multiple cameras, which remember, this device works as a switcher to enable you to change between camera angles very easily during the live stream. In that case, I would have it set up where the YoloBox is on a light stand and then run HDMI cables to each camera. The main thing that you need to remember is that regular passive HDMI cables do have a length limit before the signal degrades and they stop working. And this really is gonna depend on the resolution of video that you are live streaming. The YoloBox Extreme supports up to 4K live streaming, but for a wedding, I would recommend sticking with 1080p or 720p, which will be easier on your live streaming bandwidth while still giving you a high quality video that the guests of the wedding will be able to watch. If you want to stream in 4K though, you are only going to be able to use HDMI cables that are about 10 to 15 feet in length. But if you're streaming in only 1080p HD, you could get 25 to 50 feet of HDMI cable run. To get around these limitations though, if you look into purchasing fiber optic HDMI cables, these are more expensive, but can give you much longer cable links of hundreds of feet without issue. Just be aware that these cables will be more expensive. Remember, I'm linking to every single piece of gear that I recommend in the cheat sheet that's available for you to download for free in the video description. If you're planning on running a two camera setup like this, I would recommend positioning one of the cameras to the side of the ceremony, so you can show one of the couple's faces and the efficient. And then I would set up the other camera in the back of the ceremony on a tripod filming up the aisle, so you can show a bit wider view of the ceremony. With a setup like this, you will be able to cut between both angles of the ceremony, while also being careful to avoid having your cables in a place where a lot of people are gonna be stepping on them. Now, do you want to get cooler? Here is the ultimate wedding film live streaming setup in my opinion, and this is gonna be really easy for you to pull off, and it's going to look awesome. This is gonna be a three camera live streaming setup, so we're gonna have the two cameras set up, just like we talked about at the side and the back on the tripods, but for this third camera, we're gonna introduce a wireless live streaming option. So for this third camera, you're either gonna have it handheld or mounted onto a gimbal, and then you're gonna use a wireless transmission system. In this case, I've used a Axun Cinevue to wirelessly transmit the video signal from the camera to the YoloBox. So what this means is that you have a fully mobile live streaming camera. This opens up so many possibilities, and just like with all the other cameras, everything still runs into the YoloBox, which makes it super simple to run the entire live stream. Speaking of the YoloBox, this is also where we need to turn our attention to this fantastic device, because with your cameras set up and running, I'm now gonna walk you through how to use the YoloBox to tie everything together, specifically for a wedding ceremony live stream. First, you're gonna wanna turn on your cameras and the YoloBox, then you're gonna wanna set your camera to record at 30 frames per second, because I find that 30 or 60 FPS works better for live streaming than 24 FPS. Then you're gonna wanna connect the cameras to the YoloBox using HDMI inputs four through eight on the YoloBox Xtreme. I recommend using inputs four through eight, because these are the inputs that support 4K, and they should be a bit more compatible with your camera. I would only use the other three inputs that are only 1080p if you absolutely needed to. Now with all of the cameras connected, we're gonna select Enter for live streaming, and then press the red plus button to start a new live stream. We're gonna call this one Wedding Ceremony, but you can call it whatever you want. Just remember the title that you choose here is what is gonna show up on your streaming website as well, so be careful with that. You can also put in a description here, schedule the live stream if you know that the ceremony's gonna be happening at a specific time. If you want to also, you can create an event cover, which will also be the thumbnail for this live stream, and I would recommend creating a custom wedding film live stream thumbnail, and this can help make your live stream look more professional. With that done, go ahead and press Create, and here is your wedding film live stream on the left, so we're gonna tap on that. Now that is gonna open up and present you with the main live streaming interface where you can control everything. Any cameras connected should automatically be recognized, but if they aren't, just tap the Add Video Source in the bottom left and select which input the camera is connected to. As you can see, you have a ton of options here, including the option to add an image or even load a website. I would recommend creating a custom image that says something like, the ceremony will be starting soon. That way you'll have a visual to show on the live stream before you actually show one of the cameras. Now with that selected, go ahead and press the back button here, and let's talk about the main interface here. On the top left, you have a preview for your live stream. On the bottom left is gonna be all of the inputs for your different cameras that you can tap between to switch between all of them, and on the right, all of your settings can be adjusted. First, you're gonna want to go down here to the bottom right and swipe over here to the right on the menu, down here to this hourglass icon, click that, and then this is gonna be your video transitions. You're gonna wanna change this from the more generic cut to the fade option, which, notice whenever I do that, now whenever I tap between the inputs, they have a really nice smooth fade that looks really good. A little bit better than the instant cut, in my opinion. Next, on the bottom right, you're gonna see a record icon here, and then to the right of it, there's gonna be an angled bracket with an underscore. Go ahead and tap on that, and that's gonna bring up all of your streaming setting. I recommend setting your live streaming resolution to 1080p, the frame rate to 30 frames per second, and the bit rate, you should be able to leave it at four megabits per second. Then, here's where things get really cool. The YOLO Box Extreme can also record footage from the live stream, and here's where you will change those settings. I would recommend setting your recording to be in 4K, 3840 by 2160, at 30 frames per second, and then increase the bit rate up to about 50 megabits per second, and then for more settings, select H.265 as your encoding format. Then, go back down to the bottom right and tap on the record icon, and if you go up here to the top right, here is where you can select where you want your footage to be recorded. You can either have it recorded to the internal storage, which actually has a lot of space, or you can insert an SD card or a USB stick. Then, down here, you have this select recording source option with a bunch of check boxes, and this is really important. By default, the YOLO Box is gonna be set to program, which means that any time that you change between these different video inputs, it's basically just recording this video and the input that you have selected, stitching together a video that has all of these different inputs. But alternatively, you could tell the YOLO Box to record all of these different inputs if you wanted to by checking these boxes. This way, you can have all of the angles separately recorded, which is really cool. Then, you're going to want to set the recording limit time to 10 minutes, meaning that don't worry, the YOLO Box will continue recording past 10 minutes, but every 10 minutes, it's going to save each video clip and then restart the recording. So in the event that the device loses power, et cetera, you only lose 10 minutes of footage, not your entire video, which is great. And lastly, here's this really cool feature called Generate Timeline FCP XML file, which if you enable it, the YOLO Box will create a timeline file that you can import into Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or Adventure Resolve. And it will have cuts in all of the places where you change between each of the different inputs. It's basically a pre-edited wedding ceremony video, which you can use in the event that you wanted to livestream the wedding ceremony at a lower resolution and then record higher resolution ceremony files to create a ceremony video for the couple after the wedding. You could also charge more for that. It's a great way to make extra money. With that done, we need to focus on audio now, because obviously, your livestream is going to need audio of the efficient and the couple. Remember, I recommended that you get a wireless microphone system, and you're going to put the microphone on the efficient and then connect the receiver directly into the YOLO Box through the microphone input jack here at the front. With that now connected into the microphone input, we're gonna tap the speaker icon on the bottom left, and this is gonna bring up the screen that has all of the audio inputs that the YOLO Box is receiving. At the top, you have PGM, which stands for program, which is all of the inputs combined together, and down below that, you have your individual inputs labeled HDMI, as well as your microphone input, which is labeled mic. Because you're only going to be wanting to livestream audio from this one microphone, you're gonna want to turn off all of these other HDMI inputs by tapping on the on to make sure that they are grayed out. Then you can also turn off the headphone monitoring for them as well, and then you're gonna wanna make sure that the microphone setting is turned on and the program setting is turned on. Now, if you wanted to run multiple microphones, maybe you wanted one on the efficient and another on the couple, you could do that and run the other microphones into the cameras and then turn them on here as needed because they're gonna be transmitting the audio through the HDMI. But what could make things really cool is that if you wanted to make things more automated for switching between all of these different cameras, you can select the auto switch icon down here on the bottom right, which is the two cameras that have the lines between them, and then select the video follows audio checkbox, which if you select that, that's gonna make the YoloBox automatically switch to the camera where a person is speaking into their microphone. So the camera would stay on the efficient while they're speaking, but once the brighter groom begin to speak and say their vows, the YoloBox would automatically switch to their camera angle because it recognized that their microphone was picking up audio. This is some really cool advanced stuff. Let's go back over to the default though, and back to the sound settings because there's one more thing we need to talk about here. This little headphones red icon below this is how you can turn on and off your headphone monitoring. And there's a headphone jack at the bottom of your YoloBox that you can plug into to monitor the audio as it is recording. With that, the YoloBox is now set up and you are almost ready to live stream. The last thing you need to do is select the platforms icon in the bottom left, which is the one dot and it splits out into other dots. And this is where you can choose the platform that you want to stream to. The YoloBox supports Facebook, YouTube, Twitch, as well as custom RTMPs and the newer SRT format, which is really like any other live streaming website out there will support one of these formats. So you have a ton of versatility here. All you have to do is select the platform that you want to connect to and tap link, which is then going to bring up a list of your accounts. If you don't have any created tap add account and that's gonna open up Google Chrome, which will then let you sign in to your account for that specific live streaming platform, press continue, go on ahead and log in and then you can go back and it will then be linked here, which I have done for Facebook. Then whenever you want to live stream, all you have to do is enable the connection. It will then say, where do you want to live stream to? You can select your timeline or pages if it's Facebook. But with that turned on, all you have to do is tap go live and you will immediately be live streaming to that platform. But this is of course the moment where we need to turn our attention to the internet, which is the final puzzle piece of live streaming. You need to have a consistent and reliable internet connection to make the live stream work. Thankfully, the YoloBox has you covered because it easily offers the most connection options. Keep in mind that every wedding ceremony is gonna be different. But even if you're filming a lotement in the middle of nowhere or a barn in the country with sketchy wifi, let me walk you through how you can make sure you have a steady connection. First, you can connect the YoloBox to wifi, which it has antennas on the side to boost its ability to send and receive signal. If that doesn't work though, or if you want a stronger connection, there is also an ethernet port, which like I said earlier in this video, I have run an ethernet cable hundreds of feet before at a wedding to make sure I had a stable live stream connection. And that is a great option as long as the venue has a router and a location that you can hopefully access and run the cable from. Lastly though, here is where the YoloBox steps above all of the other live streaming options because it also has a built-in SIM card slot where you can insert a data SIM card from any cell phone company and use that to live stream as well. So if you are filming a ceremony out in the middle of nowhere, you can still live stream. But I hear you're telling me, "Matt, weddings are a really important event "where anything could go wrong." And maybe the wifi will work before the ceremony, but once all the guests arrive, they'll try to connect to it and the signal will fail. Or maybe you're running an ethernet connection and the couple's dog, who they wanted to have in the ceremony, chews through the cable, or there's a solar flare and the cellular connection stops working. Well, can you tell I'm paranoid? Some of these things have happened to me before, but that is where one of the strongest and best features of the YoloBox comes in, in my opinion, and that is a feature called network bonding, where you don't have to just use one of these connections, wifi, internet, or cellular. You can use all three of them at the same time, combining them together to make your live stream as smooth and as air free as possible. This is an extra paid feature from YoloBox, but in my opinion, it is completely worth it for the peace of mind it will give you. And it also makes sense they're charging for it because it takes a lot of server work behind the scenes to pull it off. YoloLive, the company that makes YoloBox, has servers all over the world. And whenever you use this network bonding feature, they will route all of the wifi, ethernet, and cellular traffic through their servers, which will combine all of the data. So if the dog chews through the ethernet cable, the wifi will take over. And if the wifi craps out, it will fall back to cellular. All three of these connections will work together, and it all happens behind the scenes without you needing to really do anything. You just turn on this feature and it works, which is great. With that, as I hope I've been able to show you in this video, live streaming a wedding can be dramatically simpler than you may have ever thought it could be if you are using the proper tools. If you want to simplify your live streaming setup for weddings, a YoloBox makes a lot of sense and can help you save time and money on a switcher and laptop. Plus, whenever you add in the extra features like great battery life that will easily last through even the longest wedding ceremony and network bonding to help guarantee you have a good live stream by using wifi, ethernet, and cellular together, this device is a no-brainer. I will link to the YoloBox Extreme that I used here in this video, as well as other YoloBoxes that I recommend for smaller setups in the video description. And I've also linked to my wedding film live streaming cheat sheet down below, as well for you to download completely for free if you want to see all of the other live streaming gear that I recommend. Thanks so much for watching. Please like this video if you enjoyed it. Subscribe if you loved it and you want to see more videos about filmmaking and video editing, and have a great day.
